options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: required_subgame_completions
options.description: How many subgames must be completed for the game to be considered complete.

options.name: required_subgames
options.description: Which subgames are required to be completed for the game to be considered complete.

options.name: starting_subgame
options.description: The subgame that will be unlocked by default.

options.name: included_subgames
options.description: Which subgames should be included as locations.

options.name: consumables
options.description:
Adds the specified consumables to the location pool. Options are Maxim Tomato, 1-Up,
and Invincibility Candy.

options.name: essences
options.description: Adds Copy Essence pedestals to the location pool.

options.name: the_great_cave_offensive_required_gold
options.display_name: The Great Cave Offensive Required Gold
options.description: Required amount of gold that is needed in order to complete The Great Cave Offensive

options.name: the_great_cave_offensive_excess_gold
options.display_name: The Great Cave Offensive Excess Gold
options.description: How much of the excess gold should be kept within the multiworld.

options.name: the_great_cave_offensive_gold_thresholds
options.display_name: The Great Cave Offensive Gold Thresholds
options.description:
What percent of the required gold is required before allowing access to
Crystal/Old Tower/Garden areas in The Great Cave Offensive

options.name: milky_way_wishes_mode
options.description:
Determines how Marx is unlocked in Milky Way Wishes.
Local: Marx is unlocked after completing the 7 main planets
(Floria, Aqualiss, Skyhigh, Hotbeat, Cavios, Mecheye, Halfmoon)
Multiworld: Marx is unlocked after receiving 7 Rainbow Stars scattered across the multiworld

options.name: kirby_flavor_preset
options.description:
The color of Kirby, from a list of presets.
Can also accept a valid preset defined in `kirby_flavors`, 1/2 direct hex colors (#RRGGBB|#RRGGBB), a single hex
color and a method to derive a second color (#RRGGBB|complement/analogous/intensify), or a dict taking each Copy
Ability (and Kirby) as a key and one of the former options as the value.

kirby_flavor_preset:
    kirby: #99EEDD|analogous
    Copy: bubblegum
    Cutter: #AAEEDD

options.name: kirby_flavors
options.description:
Define custom colors for Kirby. To use a custom color, set the preset to the name defined and then define a dict of keys from "1" to
"8", with their values being an HTML hex color.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.
